definition: The key test from Heck v. Humphrey: a Section 1983 claim is barred if a judgment in the plaintiff's favor would logically require the conclusion that the plaintiff's existing criminal conviction was wrongful. The parties sharply disagreed on whether prospective relief meets this test.
examples: The central dispute was whether declaring the ordinance unconstitutional going forward 'necessarily implies' that Olivier's past conviction under the same ordinance was invalid
